section_title: INTRODUCTION TO QUALIFYING TRADE CONDITIONS
content:
Welcome back folks. This is lesson 2.2 of the January 2017 ICT mentorship. We're gonna be looking at
qualifying trade conditions with the 10-year yields.

section_title: 10-YEAR NOTE SEASONAL TENDENCY AND JUNE 2015 EXAMPLE
content:
Okay, the previous teaching 2.1 we had looked at the 10-year note seasonal tenancy where it has a strong
tendency to rally in June. And we're looking at the 10-year note for September 2015 here. You can see how
during the month of June of 2015, the 10-year note did in fact make a low in June.

section_title: QUALIFYING SWINGS WITH DOLLAR INDEX FOR 2015 EXAMPLE
content:
And while the seasonal tendency is in force, how do you know the seasonal tendency is most likely going
to occur? Well, the first step is you want to start looking to qualify the swings in relationship to the Dollar
Index, and that's seen by looking at these lower lows here. So our delineation begins at the beginning of
June 2015 and subsequent lower lows.

section_title: 2015 10-YEAR NOTE PRICE ACTION AND IDEAL DOLLAR INDEX SYMMETRY
content:
It transpired after June 2015 began. We started seeing a slide in price and took out the low from May. But
individually there was five to six different candles that progressively kept making lower lows in the first half
of June, that's being delineated here by this short little trend line. Now in the Dollar Index this is going to be
ideally seen with a series of higher highs, that's how Market symmetry should be posted and delivered in
price.

section_title: ACTUAL DOLLAR INDEX ACTION AND CRACK IN CORRELATION (JUNE 2015)
content:
But let's take a look at what actually happens in the Dollar Index at that same moment. We can see that the
Dollar Index for June 2015, immediately to the right or after June 1st trading day, we were making lower
highs.

section_title: TRADE IDEA CONFIRMATION VIA INTEREST RATE DECLINE (JUNE 2015)
content:
So this is a crack in correlation, and we have confirmation now there is a trade idea unfolding in the 10-year
Treasury Note against the Dollar Index. It's further confirmed by seeing the interest rate Market declining at
that time. As the yield declines the Futures price on a 10-year note was actually rallying.

section_title: CONSOLIDATION ACROSS MARKETS IN 2015
content:
Note also did the consolidation that the yield stayed in, which also led to a consolidation in Dollar Index, 10
- year Treasury and foreign currencies at the same time in 2015.

section_title: JUNE 2016 SEASONAL TENDENCY AND EXPECTED DOLLAR INDEX SYMMETRY
content:
Okay, we're looking at the June of 2016. Again, we're looking at that seasonal tenancy. As seen here, the
market was making equal lows, so they should be seen in respect to the Dollar Index with equal highs.

section_title: DOLLAR INDEX HIGHER HIGHS AND DIVERGENCE (JUNE 2016)
content:
Here's the Dollar Index going into June 2016. Did you see here the market made higher highs? This is a
crack in correlation. The Market's shown a willingness to go higher in the Dollar Index but it was not seen in
the form of going lower on the 10-year notes.

section_title: 2016 QUALIFYING CONDITION AND INTEREST RATE CONFIRMATION
content:
That's a crack correlation there. Therefore it is a qualifying condition. There is an underlying trade
underway. This is further confirmed by visually seeing the interest rate Market seeing it decline in the 10
- year yields.

section_title: CONSOLIDATION IN 2016 AND CURRENCY MARKETS
content:
Again, notice the consolidation. While it looks like if you zoomed in really tight it would look like an uptrend,
but we're still in a rather large consolidation. And that is attributing factors to why the currency markets had
a consolidation this period of time.

section_title: 10-YEAR TREASURY NOTE MARCH 2017
content:
- LOWER HIGH
Lastly, we have the 10-year Treasury Note March contract 2017. We have a lower high. Now I know you're
looking at that big wick on the elections, but just dispel that for a moment.

section_title: 2017 MARKET STRUCTURE AND DOLLAR INDEX CONTRAST
content:
Focus on the market structure alone. Without that wick, we have a lower high formed in the first couple
days of November relative to the mid-October highs. So that should be seen supported in the Dollar Index
as well, just contrary. In fact, we see a lower low.

section_title: BROKEN SYMMETRY IN 2017 DOLLAR INDEX AND 10-YEAR NOTE
content:
So for the Dollar Index to have a lower low here, we should have seen a higher high form in the 10-year
note. That didn't happen. We see the opposite with a lower low in Dollar Index. We don't see the higher
high in 10-year Treasury Notes as you would expect to see with the lower low in Dollar Index.

section_title: IMPLICATIONS OF BROKEN MARKET SYMMETRY
content:
Again, it's going to be a mirror image of everything you see for perfect symmetry. When that symmetry is
broken, it indicates there is an underlying trend or manipulation otherwise.

section_title: OPEN INTEREST, SMART MONEY, AND YIELD IN 2017 RALLY
content:
You can also see the open interest decline in November supporting the idea that there was short covering
by the smart money, and the Dollar Index moved up as a result. Again, because the 10-year note was in a
move that was trending it had more range to go lower, that supports the trending move on a Dollar Index
where I was allowed to trade for two complete months. And you can see it's supported with the increase in
the interest rate yield on the 10-year note during that time when the dollar rallied.

section_title: MARKET SEEKING YIELD AND BLENDING WITH QUARTERLY SHIFT CONCEPTS
content:
Markets seek yield and if the interest rate's increasing, therefore the dollar has a strong tendency to rally as
well. Now as a side note, when we see these things occurring in the price, you want to be blending these
ideas with the Quarterly Shift Concepts that's been taught in this month's content.

section_title: TRADE HORIZON AND QUARTERLY SHIFT APPLICATION
content:
So if we're looking for the next three to four months potential next swing, not that we're holding for that
period of time, or our time Horizon is three months out. Generally it's going to be half that time frame for the
trade setups to come to fruition and complete.

section_title: REALISTIC TRADE HORIZON AND LONG-TERM TRENDS
content:
Sometimes the trades will last longer and go a full duration of three months and sometimes a little bit
longer. But as I mentioned in this mentorship, my time Horizon is about three months. I think it's pretty
much realistic as you're gonna get because long-term trends, while they all generally stay in the long-term
trending environment, they can reverse. And if it's in a long-term uptrend you don't want to be trying to pick
the top. So it's important that I remind you every time we talk about this that my focus primarily is looking at
a time Horizon of three months with the expectation I'm going to trade with a position that may go up to
three months, but rarely admittedly I don't hold that long, usually half that time frame.

section_title: SMT DIVERGENCES AND INTEREST RATE TRIADS FOR QUALIFICATION
content:
But you can go in and look at these times when the seasonal will align, and there are qualifying SMT
divergences between the Dollar Index and the 10-year note. When they have that pattern there you can
also qualify it with an interest rate Triad. Or you can go into a Forex currency pair and look for the SMT
Divergence against the Dollar Index that way. Those ideas blended together, not that you need both of
them, but one of them will give you a qualification for a trade idea that may be unfolding for a quarterly shift.

section_title: BENEFITS OF ALIGNING WITH HIGHER TIME FRAME ORDER FLOW
content:
By getting in sync with the marketplace like this and viewing a higher time frame charts with these tools in
alignment, it gives your trades a high probability and it filters out a lot of the noise. And even if you don't
trade in a long-term position capacity, you can use these ideas to get in sync with higher time frame Trend
or higher time frame order flow. And by doing that, it puts all your swing trade, short-term trade, day trade,
and Scouts in line with institutional workflow. And with that, it's very hard to go wrong. Not perfect, it doesn't
negate the opportunity for you to lose money, but it certainly does put the odds in your favor. Until next time,
I wish you good luck and good trading.
